message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Nobu Restaurant (Indian Wells) (Indian Wells)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 148 reviews; Restaurant Guru aggregates ~974; Google rating reported ~4.2 [UNVERIFIED exact count]
- Sentiment: mostly positive with mixed notes on price/noise/service consistency
- Longevity: Originated as a 2014 BNP Paribas Open pop-up; permanent fixture since February 2023 (~3 years as permanent)
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life editorial feature ('feels like a secret sushi club')","Featured on OpenTable Best Fine Dining Palm Springs/Palm Desert Cities list","Part of Chef Nobu Matsuhisa global brand (Matsuhisa is a James Beard Award winner, Best California Chef 2002 — credential belongs to chef/brand, not specifically this location)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best fine dining Indian Wells', 'Japanese restaurant Palm Desert/Indian Wells', 'date night Indian Wells', 'sushi Coachella Valley'. Dominates Indian Wells fine-dining query results alongside The Nest and Vue Grille & Bar.
- Site structured data: Official site noburestaurants.com/indian-wells presents menu structure and address but no visible schema.org markup detected in fetched content. Hours buried behind navigation rather than surfaced directly. Social handles (Facebook/Instagram @nobuindianwells) present. Brand-level site architecture rather than location-optimized for agent parsing.
- Visibility gap score: 2
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Nobu Matsuhisa James Beard Award Best California Chef 2002"

Links: [{"title":"Nobu Matsuhisa | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://archive.jamesbeard.org/chef/nobu-matsuhisa"},{"title":"Nobu Matsuihisa - California Museum","url":"https://californiamuseum.org/inductee/nobu-matsuihisa/"},{"title":"NOBU: Chef Nobu Matsuhisa","url":"https://www.tokyojournal.com/sections/travel-food/item/969-nobu-280.html"},{"title":"Nobu Matsuhisa - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobu_Matsuhisa"},{"title":"Nobu Matsuhisa | Chef | Reporter Gourmet S.r.l.","url":"https://reportergourmet.com/en/chef/302-nobu-matsuhisa"},{"title":"10 Pioneers Who Served Up the California Cuisine Movement","url":"https://californialocal.com/localnews/statewide/ca/article/show/34609-10-pioneers-of-california-cuisine/"},{"title":"A 3.7 Yelp-Rated Michigan Sushi Joint Won A James Beard Award. Should It Have?","url":"https://thehunger.substack.com/p/a-37-yelp-rated-michigan-sushi-joint"},{"title":"NOBU MATSHUHISA | Ambassadors of Taste","url":"https://www.ambassadorsoftaste.com/nobu-matshuhisa"}]

Based on the search results, I can provide you with information about Nobu Matsuhisa and the James Beard Awards:

Nobu Matsuhisa has never won a "best chef" award from the James Beard organization. However, he was a nominee for Outstanding Chef in 2002 (among other years from 1997-2006). 

In 2002, Matsuhisa received an induction into Who's Who of Food and Beverage in America by the James Beard Foundation. Additionally, in 1995, he received the James Beard Foundation Award, which was for Best New Restaurant, not a "best chef" category award.

So while Nobu Matsuhisa was nominated for Outstanding Chef by the James Beard Foundation in 2002, he did not win a "Best California Chef" award that year—the search results indicate he has never won a James Beard "best chef" award, despite being nominated multiple times.

Links: [{"title":"Nobu Indian Wells | BNP Paribas Open","url":"https://bnpparibasopen.com/nobu-indian-wells"},{"title":"Nobu Indian Wells Dining Package | BNP Paribas Open","url":"https://bnpparibasopen.com/nobu-ticket-package"},{"title":"News | BNP Paribas Open","url":"https://bnpparibasopen.com/news/nobu-indian-wells-to-open-year-round-to-the-public-beginning-february-13/"},{"title":"BNP Paribas Open | Indian Wells","url":"https://bnpparibasopen.com/"},{"title":"Nobu Indian Wells | Indian Wells CA | Facebook","url":"https://www.facebook.com/NobuIndianWells/"},{"title":"BNP Paribas 2025 Insider’s Guide | Discover Palm Desert","url":"https://discoverpalmdesert.com/bnp-paribas-insiders-guide"},{"title":"BNP Paribas Open Event Indian Wells Tennis Garden Restaurants Nobu","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/nobu-helps-christen-new-bnp-paribas-open-tennis-stadium/"},{"title":"Full Lineup of 2023 On-Site Activities Announced","url":"https://bnpparibasopen.com/news/full-lineup-of-2023-on-site-activities-announced-new-era-of-entertainment-to-greet-fans-in-tennis-paradise"},{"title":"FAQs | BNP Paribas Open","url":"https://bnpparibasopen.com/visit/faqs"},{"title":"The Ultimate Guide to Attending the BNP Paribas Open in Indian Wells, California - Kaylchip","url":"https://kaylchip.com/bnp-paribas-open-indian-wells/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Nobu Indian Wells:

## Opening and Timeline

Nobu Indian Wells was announced to open year-round to the public beginning February 13, 2023 (according to an official BNP Paribas Open announcement dated February 6, 2023).

## Pop-Up History and Context

Stadium 2, a new 8,000-seat venue, opened along with a bar and three pop-up restaurants — Nobu, Chop House and Piero's Pizza Vino. This was actually part of the 2023 BNP Paribas Open expansion.

However, the search results also reveal an earlier connection: This was the first foray into the desert for the acclaimed Japanese restaurant Nobu, which has locations around the world from Beijing to Budapest and Melbourne to Moscow. The chain has also done pop-up locations in the Hamptons and in Monte Carlo during the Grand Prix.

## Current Status

World-famous Japanese restaurant Nobu once again delights fans with their famous Japanese fusion cuisine and dine with a pristine view of the on-court action at the 2023 tournament. The restaurant continues to operate as one of the sit-down dining options at the BNP Paribas Open.

content.text: Important finding: the James Beard claim appears wrong. Let me verify more carefully.

input.prompt: What James Beard Award wins or nominations did Nobu Matsuhisa receive? List years and categories. Did he win Best California Chef in 2002?

input.query: Nobu Matsuhisa "Best Chef California" 1989 James Beard winner

content.content:
# Nobu Matsuhisa's James Beard Awards

Based on the webpage, here are his nominations and wins:

**Nominations:**
- Outstanding Chef (1997, 1999, 2000, 2001, 2002, 2003, 2004, 2005, 2006)
- Cooking from a Professional point of view (2006)

**Wins:**
- "Best New Restaurant 1995"
- "Who's Who of Food & Beverage in America 2002"

**Regarding your question:** No, there is no record of a "Best California Chef" award in 2002 listed on this page. The only 2002 award mentioned is the "Who's Who of Food & Beverage in America" win.

Web search results for query: "Nobu Matsuhisa "Best Chef California" 1989 James Beard winner"

Links: [{"title":"Nobu Matsuihisa - California Museum","url":"https://californiamuseum.org/inductee/nobu-matsuihisa/"},{"title":"NOBU: Chef Nobu Matsuhisa","url":"https://www.tokyojournal.com/sections/travel-food/item/969-nobu-280.html"},{"title":"Nobu Matsuhisa | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://archive.jamesbeard.org/chef/nobu-matsuhisa"},{"title":"Nobu Matsuhisa - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobu_Matsuhisa"},{"title":"Nobu Matsuhisa | Chef | Reporter Gourmet S.r.l.","url":"https://reportergourmet.com/en/chef/302-nobu-matsuhisa"},{"title":"Japanese government honors co-owner of Nobu Restaurant","url":"https://spectrumnews1.com/ca/southern-california/food/2024/11/02/japanese-government-honors-co-owner-of-nobu-restaurant"},{"title":"A 3.7 Yelp-Rated Michigan Sushi Joint Won A James Beard Award. Should It Have?","url":"https://thehunger.substack.com/p/a-37-yelp-rated-michigan-sushi-joint"},{"title":"Nobuyuki Matsuhisa: Master of Japanese Cuisine | Luxe Beat Magazine","url":"https://luxebeatmag.com/nobuyuki-matsuhisa-master-of-japanese-cuisine/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding Nobu Matsuhisa and the 1989 award:

In 1989, Nobu Matsuhisa was recognized as one of America's 10 Best New Chefs by Food and Wine magazine, not as a "Best Chef California" winner for the James Beard Foundation.

Regarding James Beard Awards, Nobu Matsuhisa has never won a "best chef" award from the James Beard organization. However, Nobu's restaurant was awarded Best New Restaurant by the James Beard Foundation in 1995, and he received nominations for Outstanding Chef by the James Beard Foundation from 1997 to 2006, but did not win in that category.

So to clarify: Nobu Matsuhisa received the Food and Wine magazine recognition in 1989, but did not win a James Beard "Best Chef California" award in that year or any other year.

content.text: Confirmed: the "Best California Chef 2002" claim is FALSE. Matsuhisa won Best New Restaurant 1995 and Who's Who 2002, never a "Best Chef" award. Now let me check the 2014 pop-up claim and the Palm Springs Life feature.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Nobu Indian Wells from Palm Springs Life and other sources:

## The "Secret Sushi Club" Ambiance

Nobu Indian Wells is located on the upper level of Stadium 2 at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden, and the arrival experience includes valet service and a golf cart ride to the base of the stadium. When arriving during off-season with dim lighting and eerie silence, the restaurant feels like a secret club hidden away in the darkness.

## About the Restaurant

Nobu is a high-end chain that began in New York as a collaboration between chef Nobu Matsuhisa, Robert De Niro, and Meir Teper, serving a menu that fuses traditional Japanese fare with South American flair. For the true Nobu experience, servers advise diners to order a mix of cold plates, hot dishes, sushi, and dessert.

## History and Status

Nobu opened as a pop-up during the 2014 BNP Paribas Open and became a permanent fixture in February 2023. Reservations are strongly recommended, especially during tennis season.

The Palm Springs Life review captures the restaurant's exclusive atmosphere and its Japanese-fusion cuisine, making it an alluring destination for fine dining in the desert.

Based on the search results, here's the current status of Nobu Indian Wells:

Nobu Indian Wells, located inside Stadium 2 at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden, is open to the public year-round and welcomes guests beyond the tournament dates. The restaurant is **not permanently closed** in 2025-2026.

The restaurant permanently opened on a year-round basis to the general public beginning on Monday, February 13, serving up its world-famous dishes outside the annual tournament dates for the first time. Regular hours are Sunday – Thursday: 5pm to 9pm and Friday & Saturday: 5pm to 10pm.

During the BNP Paribas Open tournament, Nobu accommodates guests on a first-come, first-serve basis between the hours of 11am-11pm, with reservations not accepted in advance. The 2026 BNP Paribas Open features an exclusive Nobu Indian Wells Dining Package bundling a Front Box Seat ticket in Stadium 1 with a reserved table at Nobu in Stadium 2.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "OpenTable "Best Fine Dining" Palm Springs Palm Desert Nobu Indian Wells list"

Links: [{"title":"14 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-palm-desert"},{"title":"10 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Indio | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-indio"},{"title":"15 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Indian Wells | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-indian-wells"},{"title":"The Best Restaurants in Indian Wells Right Now | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/ca/indian-wells-restaurants"},{"title":"13 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in La Quinta | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-la-quinta"},{"title":"Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Desert Hot Springs | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-desert-hot-springs"},{"title":"5 Fabulous Restaurants in Indian Wells | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/5-fabulous-restaurants-in-indian-wells/"},{"title":"Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Palm Springs, Palm Springs / Palm Desert Cities | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-palm-springs"},{"title":"Restaurants Near Indian Wells Tennis Garden | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/landmark/restaurants-near-indian-wells-tennis-garden"},{"title":"Nobu Indian Wells Restaurant - Indian Wells, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/nobu-indian-wells-2"}]

I've completed the web search for OpenTable's Best Fine Dining restaurants in the Palm Springs, Palm Desert, and Indian Wells areas. Here are the key findings:

## Notable Fine Dining Restaurants Featured:

**Nobu Indian Wells** - Experience Chef Nobu Matsuhisa's world renowned cuisine in Indian Wells, overlooking Stadium 2 in the Indian Wells Tennis Garden with intimate ambiance. Nobu Indian Wells is rated 4.7 stars by 771 OpenTable diners.

**The Steakhouse** - The Steakhouse, recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award, earning accolades such as the AAA Four Diamond Award, the Palm Springs Life Best of the Best, and the OpenTable Diners' Choice Award.

**Mastro's Steakhouse - Palm Desert** - Mastro's Steakhouse Palm Desert provides a sophisticated dining experience, and as an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, continues to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley.

**Miro's Restaurant** - Miro's Restaurant, voted best Mediterranean – Continental fine dining restaurant in Palm Springs.

**Vicky's of Santa Fe** - Since 1989, the place for cocktails, dinner, live music, and nightlife in Indian Wells, has been recognized for its award-winning American cuisine and is consistently awarded "Best Hot Spot" and "Best Live Music Venue" by OpenTable diners.

The search results show multiple OpenTable curated lists for different areas in the region, with these restaurants frequently appearing as top recommendations for fine dining.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: OpenTable Indian Wells Fine Dining list exists and includes Nobu. The OpenTable rating is shown as 4.7 (not 4.2 as Google) — different platforms. Let me finalize.

confirms: Originated as a 2014 BNP Paribas Open pop-up — corroborated by multiple sources including Palm Springs Life and 10sballs.com (2014 coverage)
Permanent fixture since February 13, 2023 (~3 years as permanent) — BNP Paribas Open official announcement dated Feb 6, 2023 confirms public year-round opening Feb 13, 2023
Palm Springs Life editorial feature 'feels like a secret sushi club' — verified via palmspringslife.com URL 'nobu-at-indian-wells-tennis-garden-feels-like-a-secret-sushi-club'
Featured on OpenTable Best Fine Dining Palm Springs/Palm Desert Cities list (Indian Wells page) — confirmed via OpenTable feature URLs
Yelp review count of 148 — confirmed by current Yelp listing 'NOBU - INDIAN WELLS' updated June 2026
Nobu Matsuhisa is associated with the James Beard Foundation — he won Best New Restaurant 1995 and Who's Who of Food & Beverage in America 2002
contradicts: Claim that 'Matsuhisa is a James Beard Award winner, Best California Chef 2002' is FALSE. James Beard Foundation archive shows Matsuhisa was NOMINATED for Outstanding Chef in 2002 (and 1997-2006) but never won that or any 'Best Chef California' award. His actual JBF wins are: Best New Restaurant (1995) and Who's Who of Food & Beverage in America (2002). The 'Best California Chef 2002' credential does not exist in the JBF record.
flagged_as_unverified: Restaurant Guru aggregate of ~974 reviews — not independently verified
Google rating ~4.2 — not independently verified (OpenTable shows 4.7 from 771 diners, suggesting platform variance)
Sentiment 'mostly positive with mixed notes on price/noise/service consistency' — not verified through review sampling
Query dominance for 'best fine dining Indian Wells' alongside The Nest and Vue Grille & Bar — not directly verified via SERP test
Structured data assessment / schema.org absence on noburestaurants.com/indian-wells — not independently fetched/inspected
Visibility gap score of 2 and convertibility tier 'anchor-reference' — methodology-internal, not externally verifiable
input.notes: Major refutation: the specific JBF credential cited ('Best California Chef 2002') is incorrect — Matsuhisa's actual JBF record per archive.jamesbeard.org/chef/nobu-matsuhisa is Best New Restaurant 1995 and Who's Who 2002, with multiple Outstanding Chef nominations but no win. The credential should be restated accurately to avoid an audit-breaking error. Operating history (2014 pop-up, Feb 2023 permanent) and Palm Springs Life feature are solidly verified. Note: a stray 'NOBU - CLOSED' Yelp duplicate listing exists at 78200 Miles Ave but the active listing (same address, 148 reviews) is current — the restaurant is operating.
